role: A self-wiring interaction binder that sits between the gallery rendering surface and the state-writing fork, turning rendered elements into annotatable targets and emitting annotation/decision events.
capabilities: Listen for gallery:rendered and decision:rendered to start binding
Walk a rendered mount and bind anchorable elements by derived address
Attach an annotation strip with comment, favour, and reaction buttons
Open a comment-type picker and emit comment annotations
Emit reaction and favour annotations as gallery:verb annotate events
Emit legacy gallery:direction alias for backward compatibility
Validate and emit decision_take verbs via decide()
Expose window.galleryBinder API for explicit binding
substance_why: The file implements concrete item factories, DOM binding, event routing, canonical-address validation, and self-wiring listeners, despite comments framing it as a contract/reference.
interactions_present: Click comment button toggles the comment picker panel
Click reaction stamp button emits a reaction and appends a visual stamp
Click favour button prompts for a score and emits a favour
Click send in comment picker emits a comment
Window listens to gallery:rendered and decision:rendered to bind elements
decide() callable externally to emit a validated decision_take
interactions_missing: No keyboard/escape handling to close the comment picker
No cancel/close affordance in the comment picker beyond re-clicking comment
No input validation for the favour prompt
No debounce or throttle on rapid reaction/favour clicks
No cleanup or unbinding when rendered content is replaced
No accessibility labels/roles on the injected strip buttons
No consumer handling for gallery:verb responses or errors
No actual decision-option UI wiring inside the binder
performance_risks: querySelectorAll over a broad semantic selector on every render can scan a large DOM
Appends a separate annotation strip to every anchorable element, multiplying DOM nodes
Attaches per-element click listeners instead of using event delegation
Global window listeners are never removed, leaking on repeated module loads
innerHTML is used to build strips and picker panels repeatedly
reuse_why: Keep the address-keyed binding contract, event shapes, and canonical-address guard, but rebuild the inline DOM UI, prompts, and listener management for the redesigned gallery.
decomposition_hints: Split address derivation/canonical validation into a dedicated address module
Split annotation item factories from event emission/routing
Extract the comment picker UI into a reusable component
Extract decision_take handling into its own binder/module
Separate event subscription lifecycle from binding logic
Move icon set and markup templates into a gallery-owned skin/component layer
